---
title: "Welcome"
title-block-banner: true
page-layout: article
---

# Welcome {.unnumbered}

<div class="cover-hero">
<h1>DS-MLOps Path</h1>
<p>A structured, project-based path from Python fundamentals to production-ready data science, applying software engineering standards across the full data science lifecycle.</p>
<p class="cover-author">By <a href="https://sambaiga.github.io/" style="color:#38BDF8;font-weight:600">Anthony Faustine</a> &nbsp;<a href="https://github.com/sambaiga" title="GitHub" style="color:#94A3B8"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>&nbsp;<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/sambaiga" title="LinkedIn" style="color:#94A3B8"><i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i></a>&nbsp;<a href="mailto:sambaiga@gmail.com" title="Email" style="color:#94A3B8"><i class="bi bi-envelope-at"></i></a></p>

<div class="cover-stat-row">
<div class="cover-stat">
  <span class="stat-value">10</span>
  <span class="stat-label">Parts</span>
</div>
<div class="cover-stat">
  <span class="stat-value">20+</span>
  <span class="stat-label">Chapters</span>
</div>
<div class="cover-stat">
  <span class="stat-value">3</span>
  <span class="stat-label">Complete</span>
</div>
</div>

<a href="tutorials/01-python-basics/01-python-core.ipynb" class="btn-cover btn-primary-cover"><i class="bi bi-play-fill"></i> Start with Part 1</a>
<a href="tutorials/02-dev-tools/00-vscode-setup.qmd" class="btn-cover btn-secondary-cover"><i class="bi bi-gear-wide-connected"></i> Jump to Part 3</a>
</div>

::: {.callout-note icon=false #callout-guide}
## How this book works

Every chapter is a Jupyter notebook or QMD file meant to be **run top to bottom**, not just read. Along the way you will see four recurring markers:

| Marker | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| <i class="bi bi-info-circle-fill" style="color:#0369A1"></i> **Key Concept** | The core idea, precisely defined |
| <i class="bi bi-puzzle-fill" style="color:#009E73"></i> **Activity** | Hands-on challenge: fill in the `...` yourself before moving on |
| <i class="bi bi-bug-fill" style="color:#DC2626"></i> **Common Mistake** | A bug pattern worth recognising on sight |
| <i class="bi bi-lightbulb-fill" style="color:#7C3AED"></i> **Pro Tip** | A production-grade habit |
:::

<p class="section-label">What's inside</p>

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-complete">
<span class="part-badge badge-complete">Complete</span>

### <i class="bi bi-braces-asterisk"></i> Part 1: Foundations

Language core, control flow, professional patterns, NumPy, and data visualisation with Matplotlib, Lets-Plot, and Seaborn.

- [01: Python Core](tutorials/01-python-basics/01-python-core.ipynb)
- [02: Control Flow](tutorials/01-python-basics/02-control-flow.ipynb)
- [03: Python Patterns](tutorials/01-python-basics/03-python-patterns.ipynb)
- [04: NumPy](tutorials/01-python-basics/04-numpy.ipynb)
- [05: Matplotlib and Its Ecosystem](tutorials/01-python-basics/05-matplotlib.ipynb)
- [06: Grammar of Graphics with Lets-Plot](tutorials/01-python-basics/06-lets-plot.ipynb)
- [07: Data Storytelling and House Style](tutorials/01-python-basics/07-data-storytelling.ipynb)
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-complete">
<span class="part-badge badge-complete">Complete</span>

### <i class="bi bi-table"></i> Part 2: Data Analysis

Pandas 3, Polars, time series, and presentation-quality tables with Great Tables.

- [08: Pandas Core](tutorials/01-python-basics/08-pandas-core.ipynb)
- [09: Pandas Operations](tutorials/01-python-basics/09-pandas-operations.ipynb)
- [10: Combining and Reshaping](tutorials/01-python-basics/10-combining-reshaping.ipynb)
- [11: Time Series](tutorials/01-python-basics/11-time-series.ipynb)
- [11: Polars and the Expression API](tutorials/01-python-basics/11-polars.ipynb)
- [12: Presentation Tables with Great Tables](tutorials/01-python-basics/12-great-tables.ipynb)
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-complete">
<span class="part-badge badge-complete">Complete</span>

### <i class="bi bi-gear-wide-connected"></i> Part 3: Dev Tools

VS Code, `uv`, `ruff`, type annotations, git, pytest, pre-commit, Pydantic, and Pandera.

- [12: IDE Setup with VS Code](tutorials/02-dev-tools/00-vscode-setup.qmd)
- [13: Project Setup with uv](tutorials/02-dev-tools/01-uv-project-setup.qmd)
- [14: Code Quality with ruff](tutorials/02-dev-tools/02-code-quality-ruff.qmd)
- [15: Type Annotations](tutorials/02-dev-tools/03-type-annotations.ipynb)
- [16: Git and GitHub](tutorials/02-dev-tools/04-git-github.qmd)
- [17: Testing with pytest](tutorials/02-dev-tools/05-pytest-testing.qmd)
- [18: Automation with pre-commit](tutorials/02-dev-tools/06-pre-commit-automation.qmd)
- [19: Data Validation with Pydantic](tutorials/02-dev-tools/07-pydantic-validation.ipynb)
- [20: Schema Validation with Pandera](tutorials/02-dev-tools/08-pandera-schema-validation.ipynb)
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-coming-soon">
<span class="part-badge badge-coming-soon">Coming soon</span>

### <i class="bi bi-cpu"></i> Part 4: Classical ML

Scikit-learn pipelines, feature engineering, hyperparameter tuning with Optuna, and model evaluation.
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-coming-soon">
<span class="part-badge badge-coming-soon">Coming soon</span>

### <i class="bi bi-diagram-3"></i> Part 5: Deep Learning

Neural networks, PyTorch fundamentals, training loops, and applied deep learning for structured and unstructured data.
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-coming-soon">
<span class="part-badge badge-coming-soon">Coming soon</span>

### <i class="bi bi-chat-dots"></i> Part 6: LLMs

Prompting, retrieval-augmented generation, fine-tuning, and building applications on top of large language models.
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-coming-soon">
<span class="part-badge badge-coming-soon">Coming soon</span>

### <i class="bi bi-cloud-arrow-up"></i> Part 7: MLOps

Packaging, CI/CD, experiment tracking with MLflow, and serving models in production with FastAPI and Docker.
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-coming-soon">
<span class="part-badge badge-coming-soon">Coming soon</span>

### <i class="bi bi-boxes"></i> Part 8: ML System Design

Designing reliable, scalable ML systems: data pipelines, serving architectures, latency-accuracy trade-offs, and failure modes.
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-coming-soon">
<span class="part-badge badge-coming-soon">Coming soon</span>

### <i class="bi bi-activity"></i> Part 9: Production Monitoring

Drift detection, data quality monitoring, alerting, and keeping models honest after deployment.
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div class="part-card part-coming-soon">
<span class="part-badge badge-coming-soon">Coming soon</span>

### <i class="bi bi-gem"></i> Part 10: Value-driven AI

Aligning models to business objectives, measuring real-world impact, and closing the gap between model performance and organisational value.
</div>
:::

:::

<p style="margin-top:24px;color:#6B7280;font-size:0.875rem;">Parts are added chapter by chapter as each one reaches the same standard as Parts 1–3. Source code: <a href="https://github.com/sambaiga/ds-mlops-path">github.com/sambaiga/ds-mlops-path</a></p>
